title: Craft vs Google Docs for Students
meta_description: For students who want something easy to leave later, choose Google Docs when exporting or sharing introduces friction; Craft fails first for this decision.
constraint_lens: You need a notes app that works for one semester, is easy to share, and does not trap you if you switch later.

one_second_verdict.summary: Best for students who want something easy to leave later.
one_second_verdict.reason: Craft fails first when exporting or sharing introduces friction.

verdict.summary: Google Docs wins for students who need to submit assignments and collaborate quickly. Files live in a standard document format, sharing is link-based, and teachers often expect Docs or Word files. Craft requires exporting to PDF or Word before submission, which adds steps and formatting risk. If exporting or sharing introduces friction, Craft fails first.
verdict.decision_rule: If exporting or sharing introduces friction, Craft fails first.

sections.heading: Why Google Docs fits Students better
sections.content: Google Docs fits this student because easy exit changes what good note-taking looks like. It affects how fast you can share work now, how much rework is needed later, and whether the tool feels like a temporary help or a sticky dependency. Google Docs wins by keeping the switching cost lower.

bullets.point: Craft can still win if you are willing to invest in its model for deeper capability
bullets.why_it_matters: The higher switching cost may be worth it when the tool is also solving a more ambitious note problem.

bullets.point: Craft may support richer knowledge work than the simpler option
bullets.why_it_matters: That matters when leaving later is less important than deeper structure now.

bullets.point: Craft becomes more attractive when long-term fit outweighs easy exit
bullets.why_it_matters: The tradeoff only breaks once the user values flexibility later more than depth today.
You can send a link and edit together instantly without extra accounts. For group projects, this removes setup steps and avoids format confusion.

bullets.point: Native support for Word file format and direct downloads
bullets.why_it_matters: You can download as .docx or PDF in one click. This matches most submission portals and reduces the risk of formatting errors.

bullets.point: Commenting and suggestion mode built into the editor
bullets.why_it_matters: Teachers and classmates can leave inline comments without changing the main text. This makes revisions straightforward during short-term academic use.

sections.heading: Where each tool can break down

items.fails_when: Google Docs becomes too plain if the user is ready to commit to a heavier note model for deeper capability.
items.what_to_do_instead: Choose Craft if easy exit is no longer the main constraint.

items.fails_when: Craft breaks down when exporting, sharing, or leaving later becomes harder than the note problem itself.
items.what_to_do_instead: Choose Google Docs when lower switching cost matters more.

sections.heading: When this verdict might flip
sections.content: This can flip if easy exit stops being important and the user is ready to commit to a deeper note system for the long haul. Then Craft may be the better fit.

sections.heading: Quick decision rules
rules: Choose Google Docs if easy sharing or leaving later matters now.
Choose Craft if long-term depth matters more than switching cost.
Avoid Craft when lock-in is becoming part of the problem.

faqs.q: Which tool better matches this priority?
faqs.a: Google Docs fits this need better because Google Docs real-time collaboration through shared links. Craft fails first when exporting or sharing introduces friction.

faqs.q: When should I choose Craft instead?
faqs.a: Choose Craft over Google Docs when easy exit is no longer the main constraint. Otherwise, Google Docs remains the better fit for this comparison.

faqs.q: What makes Craft fail first here?
faqs.a: Craft fails first here when exporting or sharing introduces friction. That is the point where Google Docs becomes the stronger pick.

faqs.q: Is this verdict only about one feature?
faqs.a: No. Google Docs beats Craft because Google Docs real-time collaboration through shared links, while Craft loses once exporting or sharing introduces friction.
